description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with a weathered, uneven surface and visible chisel marks, suggesting it is quite old. The front face features a recessed rectangular panel with faint, partially eroded inscriptions or engravings, possibly of historical or commemorative significance. The stone is held in place by four metal brackets at the corners, indicating it is being preserved or displayed, likely in a public or protected setting. The sides and back are irregular and rough, contrasting with the more finished front face. The coloration is predominantly a pale, sandy beige with hints of reddish-brown, typical of limestone or sandstone. The block appears solid and heavy, with no visible cavities or openings.
description: A weathered, inscribed stone block with a recessed panel on the front, secured by metal brackets and showing rough, irregular sides and back.
description_view_0: The front view shows the recessed panel with faint inscriptions and all four metal brackets securing the block.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the rough, unworked stone texture and the thickness of the block.
description_view_2: The back view displays an irregular, unfinished surface with no inscriptions or markings, and the metal brackets are visible at the top.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the stone's rough, natural edge and the depth of the block.
attribute_location_context: historic urban plaza or archaeological site
attribute_installation_method: secured with metal brackets on a display base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface is heavily weathered with eroded inscriptions and chipped edges, indicating long-term exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Yes, there are faint inscriptions or engravings on the front recessed panel.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated and displayed as a standalone artifact
